bank: Headline with more emotional or sensational wording gets more clicks than the other.
Headline that uses numbers or lists gets more clicks than the one that does not.
Headline with a question format gets more clicks than a declarative headline.
Headline that implies exclusivity or breaking news gets more clicks than a neutral headline.
Headline that is shorter and easier to read gets more clicks than a longer, complex headline.
Headline that includes a well-known celebrity or brand name gets more clicks than one without.
Headline that uses active voice gets more clicks than one using passive voice.
Headline that creates curiosity by leaving out key details gets more clicks than one that fully explains the news.
Headline that uses informal or conversational language gets more clicks than one with formal or technical language.
Headline that references current trends or popular culture topics gets more clicks than one that does not.
Headline that uses metaphors or vivid imagery gets more clicks than one using plain language.
Headline that personalizes the news by addressing the reader directly gets more clicks than one that is impersonal.
Headline that includes a surprising or counterintuitive statement gets more clicks than a straightforward headline.
Headline that highlights potential benefits or positive outcomes gets more clicks than one focusing on risks or negatives.
Headline that uses humor or wit gets more clicks than a serious or neutral headline.
